llm_explanation: Your two brief episodes of sudden left-arm numbness that fully resolved could be caused by several things — importantly, a transient ischemic attack (TIA), but also migraine aura without headache, a brief focal seizure, or a pinched cervical nerve. Because these are recurrent, focal neurologic episodes, they deserve prompt evaluation (brain imaging — preferably MRI, vascular imaging of neck arteries, ECG/heart rhythm check, and neurology review) to assess stroke risk and decide on prevention.
